• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Analysis Immigrant Experiment Data with OLS, Logistic Model and Conditional Logistic Model, Plus Clustering

Wei-Lun, Lin

2026-03-04

  • 背景: 以 OLS, logistic regression, conditional logistic regression 分析 Hainmueller et al. (2014) 的 immigrant experiment 資料，並於此文件進一步比較 1. 有無加入交互作用、2. 有無對受試者層次做 cluster-robust standand error 對於估計上的差異。

Immigrant Experiment

immigrant <- read_rds("../1-data/2-processed/immigrant_data.rds")
immigrant <- immigrant %>% 
  mutate(
    id = row_number(),
    alt_id = id + 3 - 2 * profile
  )

immigrant %>% 
  head() %>% 
  kable("html", caption="First 6 Rows of immigrant Data") %>% 
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = F) %>% 
  scroll_box(width = "100%")
First 6 Rows of immigrant Data
subject trial_id profile_id select subject_trial_id Education. Gender. Origin. ApplicationReason. Profession. JobExperience. JobPlans. PriorTripstoUS. Language. ethnocentrism profile LangPos PriorPos id alt_id

4 1 1433195 0 1 No formal education female France Seek better job in U.S. Child care provider Three to five years of job training and experience Does not have a contract with a U.S. employer but has done job interviews Entered the U.S. once before without legal authorization During admission interview, this applicant spoke [language] and used an interpreter 50 2 5 4 2 1
4 2 84805 0 2 Equivalent to completing a graduate degree in the US female Sudan Escape political/religious persecution Gardener Three to five years of job training and experience Has a contract with a U.S. employer Entered U.S. once before on a tourist visa During the admission interview, this applicant spoke fluent English 50 1 5 4 3 4
4 2 34442 1 2 Equivalent to completing fourth grade in the US female Germany Reunite with family members already in the U.S. Construction worker More than five years of job training and experience Does not have a contract with a U.S. employer but has done job interviews Never been to the U.S. During the admission interview, this applicant spoke fluent English 50 2 5 4 4 3
4 3 701166 1 3 Equivalent to completing high school in the US female Philippines Seek better job in U.S. Nurse More than five years of job training and experience Does not have a contract with a U.S. employer but has done job interviews Entered the U.S. once before without legal authorization During admission interview, this applicant spoke broken English 50 1 5 4 5 6
4 3 1091 0 3 Equivalent to completing a college degree in the US male Sudan Seek better job in U.S. Child care provider No job training or prior experience Has a contract with a U.S. employer Never been to the U.S. During the admission interview, this applicant spoke fluent English 50 2 5 4 6 5

在 immigrant experiment 中，immigrant 有9個 attributes:

Attribute Level 1 Level 2 Level 3 Level 4 Level 5 Level 6 Level 7 Level 8 Level 9 Level 10 Level 11
Education (7) No formal education Equivalent to completing fourth grade in the US Equivalent to completing eighth grade in the US Equivalent to completing high school in the US Equivalent to completing two years of college in the US Equivalent to completing a college degree in the US Equivalent to completing a graduate degree in the US - - - -
Gender (2) female male - - - - - - - - -
Origin (10) Germany France Mexico Philippines Poland India China Sudan Somalia Iraq -
Application Reason (3) Reunite with family members already in the U.S. Seek better job in U.S. Escape political/religious persecution - - - - - - - -
Profession (11) Janitor Waiter Child care provider Gardener Financial analyst Construction worker Teacher Computer programmer Nurse Research scientist Doctor
Job experience (4) No job training or prior experience One or two years of job training and experience Three to five years of job training and experience More than five years of job training and experience - - - - - - -
Job plans (4) Has a contract with a U.S. employer Does not have a contract with a U.S. employer but has done job interviews Will look for work after arriving in the U.S. Has no plans to look for work at this time - - - - - - -
Prior trips to US (5) Never been to the U.S. Entered U.S. once before on a tourist visa Has visited the U.S. many times before on tourist visas Spent six months with family members in the U.S Entered the U.S. once before without legal authorization - - - - - -
Language (4) During the admission interview, this applicant spoke fluent English During admission interview, this applicant spoke broken English During admission interview, this applicant tried to speak English but was unable During admission interview, this applicant spoke [language] and used an interpreter - - - - - - -

排除不合理的 profiles 後，共有 \(936960\) 種 potential profiles。

此外，為了方便後續圖表呈現，先對某些 attributes 的 levels 重新命名。

immigrant <- immigrant %>% 
  mutate(
    Education. = as.factor(paste0("Education.", as.integer(Education.))),
    ApplicationReason. = as.factor(paste0("ApplicationReason.", as.integer(ApplicationReason.))),
    JobExperience. = as.factor(paste0("JobExperience.", as.integer(JobExperience.))),
    JobPlans. = as.factor(paste0("JobPlans.", as.integer(JobPlans.))),
    PriorTripstoUS. = as.factor(paste0("PriorTripstoUS.", as.integer(PriorTripstoUS.))),
    Language. = as.factor(paste0("Language.", as.integer(Language.)))
  )

Model Fitting

因為在此實驗設計中，attributes 之間並不完全獨立（completely independent randomization）， 而是會因為現實限制而導致某些 profiles 不應該出現，例如：

  • 一部份的工作 （profession）會和教育（education）有關
  • 一部分移民的理由（application reason）會和原國家（origin）有關

在此種情形下，attributes 之間不再是完全獨立，而是 conditionally independent randomization。 即儘管某個 attribute A 和另一 attribute B 相關，但在給定該 attribute B 的情況下， attribute A 的分配會獨立於其他所有 attributes。 因此不能直接用只有主要效果的 models，而是要在模型中加入交互作用。

est.all.immigrant <- data.frame()
immigrant.design <- makeDesign(
  type="constraints",
  attribute.levels = list(
    "Education." = sort(unique(immigrant$Education.)),
    "Gender." = sort(unique(immigrant$Gender.)),
    "Origin." = sort(unique(immigrant$Origin.)),
    "ApplicationReason." = sort(unique(immigrant$ApplicationReason.)),
    "Profession." = sort(unique(immigrant$Profession.)),
    "JobExperience." = sort(unique(immigrant$JobExperience.)),
    "JobPlans." = sort(unique(immigrant$JobPlans.)),
    "PriorTripstoUS." = sort(unique(immigrant$PriorTripstoUS.)),
    "Language." = sort(unique(immigrant$Language.))
  ),
  constraints = list(
    list(
      "Education." = c("Education.1", "Education.2", "Education.3", "Education.4"),
      "Profession." = c("Financial analyst", "Computer programmer", "Doctor", "Research scientist")
    ),
    list(
      "ApplicationReason." = c("ApplicationReason.3"),
      "Origin." = c("Germany", "France", "Mexico", "Philippines", "Poland", "India")
    )
  )
)

form.immigrant <- select ~ Education. + Gender. + Origin. + ApplicationReason. + Profession. + JobExperience. + JobPlans. + PriorTripstoUS. + Language.
form_int.immigrant <- select ~ Gender. + JobExperience. + JobPlans. + PriorTripstoUS. + Language. + Education. * Profession. + Origin. * ApplicationReason.

immigrant: Linear Regression

根據 Hainmueller et al. (2014)， 可以用 ordinary least squares (OLS) regression 來估計 attributes 各個 levels 的 average marginal component effects (AMCE)， 即平均掉其他 attributes 的影響後，該 level 的邊際效果。 在 immigrant experiment 這樣的 2AFC 的 task 中，估計出來的數值為「該 term 出現時，選項被選擇機率的增減」。

# OLS without interaction
ols.immigrant <- fit_model(form.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "ols")
est.all.immigrant <- ols.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".OLS"), .cols = -c(attribute, term))

# OLS with clustering and without interaction
ols_clus.immigrant <- fit_model(form.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "ols", cluster = "subject")
est.all.immigrant <- ols_clus.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".OLS_clustered"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))

# OLS with interaction
# this part is completed with the `amce` function in the `cjoint` package
ols_int.immigrant <- fit_model(form.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "amce", design = immigrant.design)
est.all.immigrant <- ols_int.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".OLS_interact"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))

# OLS with clustering and interaction
ols_int_clus.immigrant <- fit_model(form.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "amce", design = immigrant.design, cluster = "subject")
est.all.immigrant <- ols_int_clus.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".OLS_interact_clustered"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))

再把 OLS 計算出來的 AMCE 另外儲存。

AME.immigrant <- est.all.immigrant

比較加入或未加入交互作用的 OLS 模型，並且同時檢驗控制或不控制 cluster-robust standard error 後的 CI：

ols.plot.immigrant <- est.all.immigrant %>% 
  filter(!grepl(":", term)) %>%  # discard interaction terms
  select(c(attribute, term, contains("OLS"))) %>% 
  select(c(attribute, term, starts_with("estimate."), starts_with("CI."))) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(attribute, term),
    names_to = c(".value", "model"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)\\.(OLS.*)$"
  ) %>%  
  mutate(
    model = fct_recode(
      model, 
      "OLS w/o clustering" = "OLS", 
      "OLS w/ subject clustered" = "OLS_clustered",
      "OLS w/ interaction\n and w/o clustering" = "OLS_interact",
      "OLS w/ interaction\n and w/ subject clustered" = "OLS_interact_clustered"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(model = fct_rev(model)) %>%
  plot_result(., n.mod = 2) +
  labs(title = "Immigrant Experiment Fitted by OLS", x = "AMCE", color="Interaction and Clustering")
  
# ggsave("./1-figure/res-immigrant-OLS.png", ols.plot.immigrant)

ols.plot.immigrant

可以發現加入交互作用後，那些有相關的 attributes 的確會有影響。

Logistic Regression

# w/o interaction
logit.immigrant <- fit_model(form.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "logit")
logit_clus.immigrant <- fit_model(form.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "logit", cluster = "subject")

est.all.immigrant <- logit.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".logit"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))
est.all.immigrant <- logit_clus.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".logit_clustered"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))

# w/ interaction
logit_int.immigrant <- fit_model(form_int.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "logit")
logit_int_clus.immigrant <- fit_model(form_int.immigrant, dat = immigrant, model = "logit", cluster = "subject")

est.all.immigrant <- logit_int.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".logit_interact"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  right_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))
est.all.immigrant <- logit_int_clus.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".logit_interact_clustered"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))

Plot the result of logistic regression.

logit.plot.immigrant <- est.all.immigrant %>% 
  select(c(
    attribute, term, (
      contains("logit") & (starts_with("estimate.") | starts_with("CI."))
    )
  )) %>% 
  filter(!grepl(":", term)) %>% 
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term))) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(attribute, term),
    names_to = c(".value", "model"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)\\.(logit.*)$"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(model = fct_rev(model)) %>%
  # mutate(
  #   model = fct_recode(
  #     model,
  #     "Logistic regression\n without clustering" = "logit",
  #     "Logistic regression\n with subject clustered" = "logit_clustered",
  #     "Logistic regression\n without clustering" = "logit_interact",
  #     "Logistic regression\n with subject clustered" = "logit_clustered",
  #   )
  # ) %>%
  plot_result(., n.mod = 2) +
  labs(title = "Immigrant Experiment Fitted by Logistic Regression", x = "Estimated Effects", color="Clustering")

# ggsave("./1-figure/res-immigrant-logit.png", logit.plot.immigrant)
logit.plot.immigrant

AME of Logistic Regression

分別計算有無交互作用模型的 average marginal effect (AME)。

Conditional Logistic Regression

# original conditional logistic regression
cl.immigrant <- fit_model(
  update(form.immigrant, . ~ . + strata(trial_id)),
  dat = immigrant, model = "clogit"
)
cl_clus.immigrant <- fit_model(
  update(form.immigrant, . ~ . + strata(trial_id)),
  dat = immigrant, model = "clogit", is_cluster = T
)

est.all.immigrant <- cl.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".CL"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))
est.all.immigrant <- cl_clus.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    std.error = robust.se,
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  select(-robust.se) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".CL_clustered"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))
cl_int.immigrant <- fit_model(
  update(form_int.immigrant, . ~ . + strata(trial_id)),
  dat = immigrant, model = "clogit"
)
cl_int_clus.immigrant <- fit_model(
  update(form_int.immigrant, . ~ . + strata(trial_id)),
  dat = immigrant, model = "clogit", is_cluster = T
)

est.all.immigrant <- cl_int.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value)) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".CL_interact"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))
est.all.immigrant <- cl_int_clus.immigrant[[2]] %>% 
  select(-c(statistic, p.value, std.error)) %>% 
  rename(std.error = robust.se) %>% 
  mutate(
    CI.lower = estimate - 1.96*std.error,
    CI.upper = estimate + 1.96*std.error
  ) %>% 
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, ".CL_interact_clustered"), .cols = -c(attribute, term)) %>% 
  left_join(est.all.immigrant, ., by = join_by(attribute, term))
cl.plot.immigrant <- est.all.immigrant %>% 
  select(c(
    attribute, term, (
      contains("CL", ignore.case = F) & (starts_with("estimate.") | starts_with("CI."))
    )
  )) %>% 
  filter(!grepl(":", term)) %>%  # discard interaction terms
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term))) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(attribute, term),
    names_to = c(".value", "model"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)\\.(CL.*)$"
  ) %>%  
  mutate(
    model = fct_recode(
      model, 
      "Conditional logistic\n w/o clustering" = "CL",
      "Conditional logistic\n w/ subject clustered" = "CL_clustered",
      "Conditional logistic\n w/ interaction \n w/o subject clustered" = "CL_interact",
      "Conditional logistic\n w/ interaction \n w/ subject clustered" = "CL_interact_clustered",
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(model = fct_rev(model)) %>%
  plot_result(., n.mod = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "immigrant Experiment Fitted by Condtional Logistic Regression",
    x = "Estimated Effects", color = "Interaction and Clustering"
  )

ggsave("./1-figure/res-immigrant-CL.png", cl.plot.immigrant)
cl.plot.immigrant

AME of Conditional Logistic Regression

## File Found! 
## Reading file...

接下來是有加入交互作用的 conditional logistic regression model。

## File Found! 
## Reading file...
AME.plot.cl <- AME.immigrant %>% 
  select(c(attribute, term, contains("CL", ignore.case=F))) %>%
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term))) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(attribute, term),
    names_to = c(".value", "model"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)\\.(.*)$"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    model = fct_recode(
      model,
      "Conditional logistic\n w/o clustering" = "CL",
      "Conditional logistic\n w/ subject clustered" = "CL_clustered",
      "Conditional logistic\n w/ interaction\n w/o clustering" = "CL_interact",
      "Conditional logistic\n w/ interaction\n and subject clustered" = "CL_interact_clustered",
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(model = fct_rev(model)) %>%
  plot_result(., n.mod = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "AME of Immigrant Experiment Fitted by Conditional Logistic Regression", 
    x = "Estimated Effects", color = "Model"
  )

# ggsave("./1-figure/AME-immigrant-CL.png", AME.plot.cl)
AME.plot.cl

Result Comparison

Logistic and Conditional Logistic Regression w/ and w/o Clustering

est.all.immigrant %>% 
  select(-contains("OLS")) %>%
  filter(!grepl(":", term)) %>% 
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term))) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(attribute,term),
    names_to = c(".value", "model"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)\\.(.*)$"
  ) %>%  
  # mutate(
  #   model = fct_recode(
  #     model,
  #     "Logistic regression\nwithout clustering" = "logit",
  #     "Logistic regression\nwith subject clustered" = "logit_clustered",
  #     "Conditional logistic\nwithout clustering" = "CL",
  #     "Conditional logistic\nwith subject clustered" = "CL_clustered"
  #   )
  # ) %>% 
  mutate(model = fct_rev(model)) %>%
  plot_result(., n.mod=4) +
  labs(
    x = "Effect estimate", col="Model",
    title = "Estimated Effects in Immigrant Experiment: \nComparison Between Logistic and Conditional Logistic"
  ) +
  theme(
      # legend.position = "bottom",
      plot.title.position = "plot"
    )

# ggsave("./1-figure/res-immigrant-BL+CL.png")

AME Comparisons

AME.plot.all <- AME.immigrant %>% 
  select(c(attribute, term, contains("interact"))) %>%
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term))) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(attribute, term),
    names_to = c(".value", "model"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)\\.(.*)$"
  ) %>%
  # mutate(
  #   model = fct_recode(
  #     model,
  #     "Conditional logistic\n w/o clustering" = "CL",
  #     "Conditional logistic\n w/ subject clustered" = "CL_clustered",
  #     "Conditional logistic\n w/ interaction\n w/o clustering" = "CL_interact",
  #     "Conditional logistic\n w/ interaction\n and subject clustered" = "CL_interact_clustered",
  #   )
  # ) %>%
  mutate(model = fct_rev(model)) %>%
  plot_result(., n.mod = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "AME of Immigrant Experiment Fitted by 3 Models", 
    x = "Estimated Effects", color = "Model"
  )

# ggsave("./1-figure/AME-immigrant-all.png", AME.plot.all)
AME.plot.all
